title: Golden Globes 2026: Snubs and Surprises
text: The 83rd Golden Globe Awards ceremony on January 11, 2026 delivered several unexpected outcomes that surprised industry observers. Teyana Taylor won Best Female Supporting Actor in a Drama for One Battle After Another, defeating frontrunner Amy Madigan from Weapons. Despite Madigan's strong showing at the Critics Choice Awards, Taylor's win caught many off guard. Director Chloé Zhao's Hamnet claimed the night's final award for Best Motion Picture Drama, defeating the heavily favored Sinners. According to reports, Zhao in fact mentioned Sinners director Ryan Coogler during her acceptance remarks, appearing visibly stunned by the victory. The director of One Battle After Another, Paul Thomas Anderson, won both Best Screenplay and Best Director, preventing a potential vote split between the two Warner Bros. films. Notable snubs included Frankenstein despite five nominations and support from the SAG Awards going home empty-handed, Wicked: For Good receiving no awards unlike its predecessor, and Bugonia, Yorgos Lanthimos' Emma Stone vehicle, failing to win despite multiple actor nominations. Sinners' screenplay lost to One Battle After Another, in another upset.
